what_it_does: An L2 acceptance test for the resolve→dispatch wire trigger. It exercises Suite.resolve_surfaced on an operator-approved build-intent and checks whether the path through drive_dispatchable → dispatch_decision → implement.launch fires or stays inert depending on COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION. It uses a stubbed suite runner and a monkeypatched launch recorder so no real subprocess or claude -p call runs, then verifies safety gates (AUTO class, empty-scope deny-all, exactly-once durable claim, operator-only off-MCP).
observations: Installs a 60-second faulthandler hang guard because the dispatch path can otherwise spawn real suite subprocesses.
Stubs s._default_suite_runner to an instant green result so the test verifies routing rather than real verification.
Defines a _LaunchReached sentinel and a make_recorder factory to capture launch arguments and short-circuit before any real subprocess.
Toggles COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION between cleared (default plan) and acceptEdits to test safe-by-default vs armed behavior.
Checks impl.permission_mode() and impl.wire_armed() at call-time after env changes.
Uses s.wire_wait_for_dispatch(timeout=30) to join background dispatch threads before asserting side effects.
Verifies exactly-once dispatch by counting decision.dispatch events with derived_from equal to the resolve seq.
Section 5 reads mcp_face/server.py source text to assert resolve_surfaced and dispatch_decision are not exposed as MCP tools.
Uses a global PASS counter and a manual check(label, cond) helper instead of a standard test framework.
standouts: The explicit 60s faulthandler guard tied to a prior 35-minute build-worker hang.
The launch-recorder pattern that proves routing reached implement.launch without firing a real claude -p.
The env-gated permission flag defaulting to plan, making the trigger inert unless deliberately armed.
The source-code grep for MCP tool exposure as an off-face assertion.
conventions: Isolated per-case FsStore in a temporary directory.
Inline docstring/comment rationale for each safety property.
Manual assertion helper and global pass counter rather than pytest/unittest.
concerns: MCP exposure is checked by grepping source text, which is brittle and may miss dynamic tool registration.
Global monkeypatch of impl.launch is restored in finally blocks, but an unhandled exception could leave state mutated.
The 60s faulthandler hard-exit can abort a debugging session.
empty_change_launch returns a hand-rolled dict that may not match the real implement.launch return contract.
The test relies on several imported symbols whose contracts are not shown in this file.
notes: A safety-oriented acceptance test that treats the resolve→dispatch trigger as a loaded gun and proves the safety is on by default.
questions: Where are permission_mode, wire_armed, drive_dispatchable, dispatch_decision, surface_build_intent, resolve_surfaced, and wire_wait_for_dispatch implemented and what are their exact contracts?
How does the background dispatch thread report failures back into the store or inbox?
What distinguishes consequence_class decision_build from source_data at the schema level?
Is the MCP source grep the only enforcement of operator-only, or is there also a runtime access-control gate?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file tests the safe-by-default and armed routing behavior described in its docstring.
apparent_themes: no real autonomous fire in tests
intention_signals: To show that the production caller connecting operator approval to autonomous dispatch is fully wired but remains inert unless the operator explicitly arms it via COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION=acceptEdits, while reusing the existing governed dispatch path unchanged.
novelty: Combines an environment-gated trigger with a launch-recorder sentinel and a faulthandler hang guard in an acceptance test, demonstrating a production routing path without ever invoking the real external tool.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that encodes the L2 wire-trigger contract and provides reusable helpers (env toggle, stubbed suite, launch recorder) for verifying the resolve→dispatch→launch path under both safe and armed postures.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test in company/tests/wire_trigger_acceptance.py that validates the L2 resolve→dispatch wire trigger, proving it stays inert under default COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION=plan and fires only when COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION=acceptEdits, using a stubbed suite runner and a monkeypatched launch recorder to avoid real subprocesses.
intention_for_embedding: To demonstrate that the production caller linking operator approval to autonomous dispatch is wired but safe-by-default and gated by an explicit environment opt-in.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter on success.
symbols.description: Takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition, and increments the module-level PASS counter; prints an ok line for each passing check.

symbols.does: Builds an isolated Suite with a temporary FsStore and a stubbed suite runner.
symbols.description: Creates a new FsStore in a temporary directory, discovers nodes, instantiates Suite, and replaces _default_suite_runner with an instant green stub so the test exercises routing rather than real suite subprocesses.

symbols.does: Removes COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION from the environment to force the safe default.
symbols.description: Calls os.environ.pop on COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION so subsequent permission_mode/wire_armed reads return the default plan posture.

symbols.does: Sets COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION=acceptEdits to deliberately arm the trigger.
symbols.description: Assigns acceptEdits to COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION so the wire trigger is considered armed for the current test case.

symbols.does: Sentinel exception raised by the fake launch to prove routing reached launch.
symbols.description: A non-LaunchError exception raised inside the recorded fake launch; because dispatch_decision only catches LaunchError, the sentinel propagates into verdict['wire_drive_error'], demonstrating the resolve→dispatch→launch path was reached without running a real subprocess.

symbols.does: Factory that returns a call list and a fake launch function.
symbols.description: Creates a list to capture launch invocations and a fake implement.launch that records the decision and repo, then raises _LaunchReached to short-circuit before any real external command.

symbols.does: The fake launch produced by make_recorder; records arguments and raises _LaunchReached.
symbols.description: Accepts decision and repo keyword arguments, appends them to the shared calls list, and raises the sentinel exception so the test can assert the launch routing was reached with the correct intent and scope.

symbols.does: A fake launch returning a deterministic no-edits result for the deny-all test.
symbols.description: Returns a dict representing a successful plan-mode run with no changed files, used in section 4 to verify that an empty declared scope prevents the build from closing to implemented even when armed.
